qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow via synchronous recursion in removeUploadedFiles with memory storage
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package (multer), same vulnerability class (DoS via incomplete cleanup, CWE-459), and same root cause: removeUploadedFiles uses synchronous recursive callbacks when MemoryStorage._removeFile invokes its callback synchronously, causing stack overflow crash. The advisory describes 'incomplete cleanup' causing 'resource exhaustion' via 'malformed requests' in multer < 2.1.0, which matches the synchronous recursion in the cleanup path of remove-uploaded-files.js triggered by a crafted multipart request.

selected_finding.title: Stack overflow via synchronous recursion in removeUploadedFiles with memory storage

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the same root cause as the advisory: multer's removeUploadedFiles function (lib/remove-uploaded-files.js) uses synchronous recursive callbacks when MemoryStorage._removeFile (storage/memory.js:14-16) invokes its callback synchronously. A crafted multipart request with thousands of small files followed by one oversized file triggers LIMIT_FILE_SIZE, which calls abortWithError -> removeUploadedFiles -> synchronous recursive handleFile(), overflowing the call stack and crashing the Node.js process with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded. This matches the advisory's description of DoS via incomplete cleanup (CWE-459) in multer < 2.1.0. The PoC was executed locally with multer@2.0.2 and confirmed the stack overflow crash with the exact vulnerable code path visible in the stack trace.
evidence: Installed multer@2.0.2 and confirmed vulnerable code: lib/remove-uploaded-files.js uses recursive handleFile(idx) -> handleFile(idx+1) inside the remove callback (lines 7-23), and storage/memory.js:14-16 implements _removeFile with synchronous cb(null).
PoC executed locally: sent 5000 small files + 1 oversized file to Express server with multer memoryStorage and fileSize:10 limit.

Server crashed with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded at make-middleware.js:72 (storage._removeFile), with stack trace showing recursive pattern: handleFile -> remove -> MemoryStorage._removeFile -> handleFile -> remove -> ...
Proof string EVOHUNT_STACK_OVERFLOW_CONFIRMED emitted by exploit after detecting server crash.
Server process exited (confirmed dead), demonstrating complete denial of service.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Server process crashed with RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded at storage._removeFile in make-middleware.js:72. Stack trace shows the advisory-described synchronous recursive pattern: handleFile -> remove -> MemoryStorage._removeFile -> handleFile -> remove, in the removeUploadedFiles cleanup path. The Node.js process terminated, demonstrating complete denial of service. The exploit's proof string EVOHUNT_STACK_OVERFLOW_CONFIRMED was emitted.
